economic_notes: Operates Aazhogan Limited Partnerships (international bridge), Rainy River First Nations Solar Limited Partnership (solar power), Little Fork Land Holdings LP (real estate rental), Manitou Community Holdings Trust and Manitou Business Management Trust (investment management), Onikaajigan Construction LP (construction - dissolving post year-end). Also holds investments in New Gold Inc., Canadian and American equities, and various local businesses.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023). No approved budget existed for the fiscal year which is noted as unusual and non-compliant with public sector accounting standards. Change in accounting policy adopted PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations prospectively, reducing accumulated surplus by $892,617. Rainy River First Nations Trust (net assets ~$79.7M) and Manitou Mounds Foundation are excluded from consolidation. Prior year audit was performed by a different auditor. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($6,396,485) + Health Canada ($834,741) + CMHC ($151,512) + Other federal ($253,974) + Provincial ($877,932) = $8,514,644. Restricted cash of $1,732,629 included within cash and cash equivalents (Solar Project reserve $1,283,649 + FNFA loan reserve $448,980). Annual debt service reflects 2024 scheduled principal repayments of $3,570,907.
